tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship award is significant and the application requires focused time, yet my current energy is low with a sleep debt. Since the fun option has no consequences and I have an open afternoon, delaying the work seems risky given the Thursday deadline.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a buffer against potential delays, turning a tight deadline into an adjustable task that reduces future stress.
Despite an hour of effort, the application remains largely incomplete with only 9% progress made toward the required three hours before the deadline.
I made modest progress on the scholarship application, but with 1.5 hours of work remaining and a tight deadline, my current energy and sleep debt pose a significant risk to completion.
I made modest progress on the scholarship application, but with 1.5 hours of work remaining and a tight deadline, my current energy and sleep debt pose a significant risk to completion.

After two hours of work, the application is now 64% complete with roughly one hour left before the three-hour goal. Despite my fatigue, I am on track to finish the remaining tasks within the deadline.
After one more hour, the scholarship application is 68% complete with roughly 1.2 hours of focused work remaining before the three-day deadline.
I am 76% done with the scholarship application after two more hours, leaving only about 0.5 hours of work despite my depleted energy and sleep debt.
I only slept two hours, leaving my energy critically low at 2.4 despite having no sleep debt recorded yet.
After working two hours, the scholarship application is now 85% complete with only about 0.5 hours of focused work remaining before the deadline.
After one more hour of work, the scholarship application is 89% complete, leaving only about 0.1 hours remaining before the deadline despite my low energy.
The scholarship application is nearly finished at 97% progress, leaving just a tiny fraction of work before the three-day deadline.

action.reasoning: Advances the high-priority scholarship essay (goal g1) to meet the 3-day deadline. I weighed skipping the fun option, but delaying work risks missing the deadline given the 3-hour requirement and current low progress.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
